skill.name: baoyu-infographic
skill.description: Creates professional infographic prompts and supporting files from source content. It analyzes content, recommends layout and style combinations, structures information, and prepares prompts for image generation.
skill.summary: Create structured infographic prompts with layout, style, aspect ratio, and language guidance.
skill.author: jimliu

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are false positives caused by Markdown code fences, inline code, design terminology, and color values. The skill is documentation-driven and has no executable scripts, but it does instruct the assistant to check fixed local preference files and save generated outputs.

medium_findings.title: Documented Local Preference File Access
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to check project and user EXTEND.md preference files, including a hidden directory under the user home path. This is a legitimate configuration feature, but it should be visible to users because it reads local files outside the immediate prompt.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines explicitly instruct Bash checks against fixed local paths. The commands are not malicious, but the filesystem behavior is real and should be disclosed.

medium_findings.title: Local Output File Creation
medium_findings.description: The workflow asks the assistant to save source content, analysis, structured content, prompts, and generated images under an infographic output directory. This is expected for the skill, but users should know that pasted source material may be persisted locally.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow clearly lists local files to create and later reports the output path. This is normal behavior for a generation skill, with no evidence of hidden writes.

low_findings.title: External Command Scanner Hits Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged many backtick and shell patterns as external command execution. Review shows these are Markdown code fences, inline option names, and fixed examples for invoking the skill or checking configuration files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation blocks and inline Markdown literals. No script file or runtime command dispatcher exists in the reviewed skill files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Scanner Hits Are Design Text
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithms in reference files. The cited content is design and instructional text, such as analysis framework wording and layout descriptions, not cryptographic API use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files are Markdown guidance for visual design and content structure. No hashing, encryption, signing, or credential-handling code was found.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Scanner Hits Are Content Categories
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged reconnaissance terms in layout and analysis documents. The reviewed lines describe infographic structures, content categories, and visual elements rather than host inspection commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is infographic taxonomy and user-facing options. No commands such as host enumeration, directory crawling, or environment collection are present.

low_findings.title: C2 Keyword Scanner Hits Are Color Values
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged C2 keywords in the chalkboard style reference. The cited lines are hex color values and color names for a visual style palette, not command-and-control indicators.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact context is a chalkboard color palette with hex values. There is no URL, network client, beacon logic, or encoded endpoint.

content.user_title: Create Structured Infographics From Source Content
content.value_statement: Dense source material is hard to turn into clear visual stories. This skill analyzes content, recommends layouts and styles, and prepares image prompts for polished infographics.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes source content for topic, data type, complexity, audience, and language.
Recommends layout and style combinations from documented layout and style galleries.
Creates structured Markdown content for infographic sections, labels, data points, and visual elements.
Combines layout guidance, style guidance, and source content into an image generation prompt.
Supports landscape, portrait, and square aspect ratios with configurable output language.
Reads optional EXTEND.md preferences from fixed project or user configuration paths.
limitations: It depends on a separate image generation capability to create the final image.
It may save pasted source material and generated artifacts to local files.
It cannot guarantee exact text rendering inside generated images.
Referenced configuration help files are not included in this package.

use_cases.title: Turn Reports Into Executive Visuals
use_cases.description: Convert business reports into dashboard, comparison, or funnel infographic prompts for stakeholder review.

use_cases.title: Create Teaching Aids From Notes
use_cases.description: Transform lessons, tutorials, and study notes into structured visual summaries with learning objectives.

use_cases.title: Prepare Social Visual Summaries
use_cases.description: Turn articles, announcements, and briefs into portrait or square infographic concepts for publication workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Infographic
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with default layout and style selection.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an infographic from this text. Recommend the best layout and style, then prepare the image generation prompt. Keep all facts exact: [paste content]

prompt_templates.title: Choose From Several Directions
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request that asks for options before generation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this source and propose four layout and style combinations. Explain the audience fit for each option, then ask me to choose one: [paste content]

prompt_templates.title: Use Specific Design Constraints
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with platform, language, and aspect requirements.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a portrait infographic in Spanish for a mobile audience. Use a technical but approachable style, preserve every statistic, and prepare the final prompt: [paste content]

prompt_templates.title: Build a Full Infographic Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request that uses files, preferences, and generated artifacts.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use /path/to/source.md, check my EXTEND.md preferences, create analysis and structured content files, recommend options, and generate the final infographic prompt.

output_examples.input: A quarterly performance brief with revenue, churn, activation, and customer support metrics.
output_examples.output: A dashboard infographic plan with highlighted KPIs, source statistics preserved exactly, and a corporate visual style prompt.

output_examples.input: A lesson about photosynthesis for middle school students.
output_examples.output: A circular-flow infographic structure with learning objectives, section labels, visual element notes, and a classroom-friendly style direction.

output_examples.input: A product comparison article covering two pricing plans and feature differences.
output_examples.output: A binary comparison infographic concept with side labels, aligned feature points, and concise text labels for image generation.

best_practices: Provide the full source text so the skill can preserve data and quotes accurately.
State audience, platform, language, and aspect ratio before final prompt generation.
Review the structured content before creating the image prompt.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to invent statistics or fill missing facts.
Do not paste confidential material unless local file persistence is acceptable.
Do not expect the final image tool to render long paragraphs perfectly.

faq.question: Does this skill create the final image by itself?
faq.answer: It prepares the analysis, structure, and prompt. A separate image generation tool creates the final image.

faq.question: Can it work with existing files?
faq.answer: Yes. The usage examples support file paths, and the workflow saves source and generated artifacts locally.

faq.question: How does it choose a layout?
faq.answer: It analyzes content type, complexity, audience, tone, and visual opportunities, then recommends matching layout and style options.

faq.question: Can I force a specific style?
faq.answer: Yes. You can request a documented layout, style, aspect ratio, and output language.

faq.question: Will it preserve exact numbers and quotes?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow repeatedly instructs the assistant to copy statistics, quotes, names, and dates exactly.

faq.question: What local files can it read?
faq.answer: It checks fixed EXTEND.md preference paths in the project and user home directory when configuration is used.
